commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Codex app-server in-flight coalescer for duplicate dynamic tool calls and wires it into both the main run path and side-question path with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +67, Tests +138. Total +205 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible, but not live reproduced here: current main executes each matching item/tool/call request through the tool bridge independently, while the PR tests model the duplicate in-flight request path. A live gateway proof is still missing.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer judgment on the narrow scope, not an automated repairable code defect.

target.title: fix(codex): coalesce in-flight dynamic tool calls
target.body:
## Summary

What changed?
- Add a Codex dynamic-tool in-flight coalescer keyed by `threadId`, `turnId`, `namespace`, `tool`, and canonicalized `arguments`.
- Wire the coalescer into both the main Codex app-server run path and the `/btw` side-question dynamic-tool path.
- Add regression coverage for stable duplicate keys, in-flight-only coalescing, and side-thread duplicate request handling.

## Why

Codex app-server can issue two `item/tool/call` requests with different `callId`s but identical tool name and arguments while the first call is still running. Because OpenClaw previously tracked execution by `callId`, both requests reached the underlying OpenClaw tool execution path. For side-effecting tools, that can duplicate external effects such as sending the same screenshot or message twice.

This change only joins duplicate calls that are still in flight within the same turn. It does not persist a dedupe cache and does not suppress later sequential repeats, so intentional repeated tool use after the first call settles still works.

## Linked context

Related issue: #80040

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Duplicate Codex dynamic-tool requests in the same thread/turn with different `callId`s but identical tool and arguments now share the first in-flight execution instead of invoking the underlying OpenClaw tool twice.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch `codex/dedupe-inflight-dynamic-tools`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.dynamic-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`
- `scripts/pre-commit/run-node-tool.sh oxfmt --write --no-error-on-unmatched-pattern extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix: Targeted Vitest passed: 3 files, 46 tests. `git diff --check` passed. The coalescer test verifies identical calls with different `callId`s execute once while later settled repeats execute again; the side-question integration test verifies two in-flight app-server requests reach the tool executor once.

Observed result after fix: The first in-flight request stores the execution promise. A duplicate request with the same semantic key receives that promise, and the entry is cleared when the promise settles.

What was not tested: A live installed gateway sending a real PowerBI or Feishu message through Codex app-server. The main app-server path and side-question path share the same coalescer implementation; the targeted tests exercise the shared coalescer and one app-server request handler integration path.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-execution.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.dynamic-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
